summary.intent: Implement CHA-52 — a data retention engine for the moltis/openclaw Rust project that automatically purges aged records from CRM interactions, message logs, and session JSONL files based on configurable per-store retention policies.
summary.outcome: Full implementation completed and committed on branch `CHA-52-data-retention-engine`: new `DataRetentionConfig` schema section, bulk delete methods on CRM/MessageLog/SessionStore, a `RetentionEngine` with background tokio loop, server wiring, and all tests/clippy/fmt passing clean.

repo: CRM `created_at` is epoch milliseconds while message log `created_at` is epoch seconds — separate cutoff helper functions required per store
`moltis-crm` is an optional feature (`#[cfg(feature = "crm")]`) in `moltis-gateway`, requiring two versions of `RetentionEngine::new()`
CronPayload types (`SystemEvent`, `AgentTurn`) are LLM-oriented and not appropriate for database maintenance; plain `tokio::spawn` + sleep loop is the right pattern for background system jobs
`moltis-web` style.css is a generated Tailwind artifact not checked into git — pre-existing compile blocker requires running `npx tailwindcss` before workspace-wide cargo check
`SqliteCrmStore` was not instantiated anywhere in `moltis-gateway` before this PR — requires fresh `SqliteCrmStore::new(db_pool.clone())` in the retention wiring
Retention metric constants are not defined in `moltis-metrics` — metrics instrumentation for the retention engine must be deferred until constants are added upstream
LINEAR_API_KEY env var is not available in MCP ctx_execute subprocesses — only accessible via Bash tool with Python urllib heredoc

code.finding: DataRetentionConfig struct added here with enabled, schedule, dry_run, and per-store retention_days fields
MemoryCrmStore uses RwLock<HashMap> for interactions; retain() on the map handles bulk delete by cutoff epoch ms
cleanup_before uses tokio::task::spawn_blocking to scan JSONL files by mtime and also removes associated media/ directories
Uses Duration::try_from(time::Duration::days(n)) to bridge the `time` crate and std::time::Duration for cutoff computation
Clippy collapsible-if lint triggered in cleanup_before — nested if conditions must be collapsed with &&

workflow: Run `cargo check --package <crate>` per-crate to isolate compile errors rather than workspace-wide checks, especially when a pre-existing unrelated crate is broken
Background task IDs from previous Claude Code sessions expire and cannot be resumed across context compaction — re-run tests rather than polling expired task IDs
Apply `cargo +nightly fmt --all` directly rather than manually fixing rustfmt issues; let the formatter do the work
Stokowski orchestration workflow: investigate → research-review → implement → implementation-review lifecycle with Linear comments as workpad

friction: LINEAR_API_KEY not available in MCP ctx_execute subprocesses — caused ~8 repeated 500 API errors before discovering the Python urllib Bash workaround
Pre-existing `moltis-web` Tailwind CSS build artifact missing blocked workspace-wide `cargo check` — required a detour to build the CSS before confirming baseline compile state
`metrics::counter!` macro invocation pattern was wrong; metrics were ultimately removed entirely since retention metric constants don't exist in `moltis-metrics`
Background test task IDs expired across the context compaction boundary, requiring full re-run of gateway and channels tests
CronPayload enum exploration was a dead end — the cron infrastructure is LLM-job-oriented and unsuitable for system maintenance tasks, requiring fallback to raw tokio::spawn loop
open_items: Memory store retention not implemented — `MemoryStore` trait was identified as needing `delete_stale_files()` in the investigation but was deferred from the implementation
Cron runs table retention not implemented — `cron_runs` table cleanup was in scope per investigation but not implemented
Metrics instrumentation deferred — retention engine has no metrics because constants are not yet defined in `moltis-metrics`; needs a follow-up ticket
Dry-run mode not fully implemented — `dry_run` config field exists but the engine likely does not skip deletes when enabled (needs verification)
SQLite VACUUM after large deletes — identified as a risk/open question in investigation, not addressed in implementation
Legal hold consideration — retention should potentially skip CRM interactions for contacts/matters under legal hold; flagged as open question, not resolved
Session archive-before-delete option — JSONL session files are deleted without backup; `archive_before_delete` config option was suggested but not implemented
PR creation and Linear workpad update — the transcript ends at commit; push, `gh pr create`, and Linear workpad update were still pending at session end
